specific_location: Squirrel Creek Road, about 2 miles east of the turn off fro the Squirrel Creek landfill.
description: This is to document a late December Grasshopper Sparrow in Colorado. Bob & Bob don't show any December records.  While looking for longspurs on 24 December on Hanover Road in El Paso County, I saw a perched sparrow on a fence so I slowed and stopped. I was shocked to see it was a flat-headed sparrow, for sure an Ammodramus so i started taking lots of photographs. Later, on the camera body, I saw it was a GRSP, based on white central crown stripe border on both sides with broad black markings. The face was dull orangish but without a strong facial pattern and the orange supraloral spot that is the clincher for the I.D. The back of the neck had thin rufous vertical streaks, the back with two white vertical rows contrasting with the rest of the back color. The bill was pink and stout. The tail was short and spiky.Behaviors: Just sat there on the top rung of a barbed wire fence and was still there when I drove away.Call: non heardPlumage: adult basic
